subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The First Three Minutes
description: Learn The First Three Minutes in one minute.

hook.text: Everything heavier than hydrogen was built in the first three minutes. The universe was a nuclear reactor.

buildup.text: Protons and neutrons collided at extreme temperatures, fusing into helium and tiny amounts of lithium.

discovery.text: About 75% hydrogen and 25% helium—the exact mix we measure in the oldest stars today. Theory matches observation.

twist.text: After three minutes, the universe cooled too fast for further fusion. Heavier elements had to wait for stars.

climax.text: Steven Weinberg's 1977 book 'The First Three Minutes' made this science accessible to millions of readers.

punchline.text: Three minutes built the raw materials. Stars did the rest.

quiz.question: What elements were created during the first three minutes after the Big Bang?
options: Hydrogen, helium, and tiny amounts of lithium
